name: Cocoa Cake
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup (1 1/2 sticks) margarine, at room temperature
Liquid sugar substitute equal to 1/2 cup sugar
3 large egg whites, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together cocoa and boiling water to blend. Set aside to cool to room temperature.
2. Cream margarine at medium speed until light and fluffy.
3. Add sweetener and vanilla to the creamed mixture, along with the cooled cocoa mixture. Mix at medium speed until well blended.
4. Beat egg whites at medium speed until foamy. Add cream of tartar and beat at high speed, gradually adding sugar, to form a meringue. Set aside for later use.
5. Stir together flour, baking soda, baking powder, salt, and cinnamon to blend well.
6. Add 1 cup water to the creamed mixture along with the flour mixture. Beat at medium speed until well blended.
7. Carefully stir batter into pans that have been greased with margarine and lined on the bottom with wax paper.
8. Bake at 350 degrees F. for 30-35 minutes, or until a cake tester comes out clean from the center of the cake and the cake pulls away from the sides of the pan.
9. Turn cake out onto a cooling rack and remove the paper. Cool to room temperature.
10. Frost with diabetic-friendly frosting at the last minute.
11. Cut into 16 equal servings.
Food Exchange per serving: 1 1/2 BREAD EXCHANGE + 2 FAT EXCHANGE
CAL: 170
CHO: 20gm
PRO: 3gm
FAT: 9gm
Low-Sodium diets: Omit salt. Use salt-free margarine and low-sodium baking powder.
